tree.setup: Your friend Xiao Chen sends you a message asking if you're free this weekend. You meet at the usual park bench to discuss plans.
tree.setup_zh: 你的朋友小陈发消息问你周末有没有空。你们在平时常去的公园长椅上碰面，讨论周末的计划。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 好久没出去玩了！这个周末你想做什么？我们可以去爬山，或者去那个新开的商场逛逛。
turns.text_pinyin: Hǎojiǔ méi chūqù wán le! Zhège zhōumò nǐ xiǎng zuò shénme? Wǒmen kěyǐ qù pá shān, huòzhě qù nàge xīn kāi de shāngchǎng guàngguang.
turns.text_en: It's been so long since we went out! What do you want to do this weekend? We could go hiking, or check out that new mall.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Suggest what to do this weekend.

options.text_zh: 我觉得爬山比较好。天气这么好，我们可以带一些吃的，在山上野餐。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ juéde pá shān bǐjiào hǎo. Tiānqì zhème hǎo, wǒmen kěyǐ dài yīxiē chī de, zài shān shàng yěcān.
options.text_en: I think hiking is better. The weather's so nice — we could bring some food and have a picnic on the mountain.
options.feedback: Enthusiastic and adds a nice detail about a picnic.
Easy-going but doesn't contribute to the planning.

options.text_zh: 我想在家休息，你自己去吧。
options.text_pinyin: Wǒ xiǎng zài jiā xiūxi, nǐ zìjǐ qù ba.
options.text_en: I want to rest at home — you go by yourself.
options.feedback: Dismissive — she's excited to spend time together.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 好主意！山上野餐一定很舒服。那我们要不要叫小李一起？她上次说也想出去走走。
turns.text_pinyin: Hǎo zhǔyi! Shān shàng yěcān yīdìng hěn shūfu. Nà wǒmen yào bú yào jiào Xiǎo Lǐ yīqǐ? Tā shàng cì shuō yě xiǎng chūqù zǒuzou.
turns.text_en: Good idea! A mountaintop picnic would be lovely. Should we invite Xiao Li too? She mentioned wanting to get out last time.

options.text_zh: 当然好啊！人多更热闹。你给她发个消息吧。
options.text_pinyin: Dāngrán hǎo a! Rén duō gèng rènao. Nǐ gěi tā fā ge xiāoxi ba.
options.text_en: Of course! The more the merrier. Send her a message.
options.feedback: Inclusive and enthusiastic — good friend energy.

options.text_zh: 不要了，就我们两个人去吧。
options.text_pinyin: Bú yào le, jiù wǒmen liǎng ge rén qù ba.
options.text_en: Nah, let's just go the two of us.
options.feedback: Not wrong, but could seem exclusive.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 太好了！那我们周六早上九点在地铁站见？记得穿运动鞋，山路不太好走。
turns.text_pinyin: Tài hǎo le! Nà wǒmen zhōuliù zǎoshang jiǔ diǎn zài dìtiě zhàn jiàn? Jìde chuān yùndòng xié, shān lù bú tài hǎo zǒu.
turns.text_en: Great! Then shall we meet at the subway station at 9 on Saturday morning? Remember to wear sneakers — the mountain paths aren't easy to walk.

options.text_zh: 好的，九点见！我带水果和面包，你带水，怎么样？
options.text_pinyin: Hǎo de, jiǔ diǎn jiàn! Wǒ dài shuǐguǒ hé miànbāo, nǐ dài shuǐ, zěnmeyàng?
options.text_en: Okay, see you at nine! I'll bring fruit and bread, you bring water — how's that?
options.feedback: Takes initiative on logistics — shows you're a good planner.

options.text_pinyin: Hǎo de, bú jiàn bú sàn.
options.text_en: Okay, see you there for sure.
options.feedback: Nice expression but doesn't add to the plan.

options.text_pinyin: Jiǔ diǎn tài zǎo le, shí diǎn xíng ma?
options.text_en: Nine is too early — how about ten?
options.feedback: Reasonable to negotiate but slightly deflates the momentum.

tree.cultural_note: Making plans with friends in China often involves multiple rounds of suggestions and confirmations. The phrase 「不见不散」 means 'we won't leave until we see each other' — a common way to confirm plans. Splitting who brings what for outings is a natural part of group planning.
